leadText: Head Charge (Japanese: アフロブレイク Afro Break) is a damage-dealing Normal-type move introduced in Generation V. It is the signature move of Bouffalant.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Contents 1 Effect 1.1 Generation VIII 1.1.1 Pokémon Sword and Shield 1.1.2 Pokémon Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl 1.2 Generation IX Effect [ edit | edit source ] Head Charge inflicts damage, and the user receives recoil damage equal to ¼ of the damage done to the target. Generation VIII [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Sword and Shield [ edit | edit source ] In Versions 1.0 to 1.1.1 of Pokémon Sword and Shield , Head Charge could not be selected in a battle. Starting with Version 1.2.0, it is usable and functions as it did in previous generations. Pokémon Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl [ edit | edit source ] Head Charge cannot be selected in a battle. Generation IX [ edit | edit source ] Head Charge cannot be selected in a battle.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description B W B2 W2 The user charges its head into its target, using its powerful guard hair. It also damages the user a little. X Y OR AS S M US UM P E Sw Sh LA The user charges its head into its target, using its powerful guard hair. This also damages the user a little. BD SP This move can't be used. It's recommended that this move is forgotten. Once forgotten, this move can't be remembered. S V The user charges its head into the target, using its powerful guard hair. This also damages the user a little.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description MD GtI UNUSED S MD It damages an enemy, but it damages you, too. * It damages an enemy, but you'll receive recoil damage, too. *

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] Despite its Pokédex entries stating that its fur absorbs damage, Bouffalant still takes recoil damage after using this move.
